steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject is "The city of Quito in Ecuador." The head noun is city (singular). The prepositional phrases "of Quito" and "in Ecuador" describe the city but do not change its number. The verb must agree with the singular subject and suit a factual, present-tense statement.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'is': singular, simple present — agrees with the singular subject "city" and correctly states an ongoing fact. ✓
  • Choice B — 'have been': plural present perfect — "have" requires a plural subject; "city" is singular. ✗
  • Choice C — 'are': plural simple present — does not agree with the singular "city." ✗
  • Choice D — 'are being': plural present continuous — both the plural form and the continuous aspect are wrong here. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
